high_complexity.description: Architectures with 20+ services get -5 points for increased complexity

medium_complexity.description: Architectures with 10-19 services get -2 points for moderate complexity

low_complexity.description: Architectures with <5 services get +3 points for simplicity

score_caps.description: Scores capped at 95% (perfection unrealistic) and floored at 0%

confidence_thresholds.description: Confidence levels for capability detection and scoring

capability_contribution_formula.example: Security with 60% encryption coverage at 0.9 confidence: 35 + (0.25 × 0.6 × 0.9 × 100) = 48.5

pattern_adjustment_rules.description: Pattern adjustments limited to ±20% to prevent extreme scores

scoring_weights.description: Relative importance of different scoring factors

validation_rules.description: Minimum requirements for generating valid scores

good.description: Core capabilities present with some gaps

security.description: Security is highest priority, weighted 20% higher

reliability.description: Reliability is high priority, weighted 10% higher

performance.description: Performance is medium priority, standard weight

cost_optimization.description: Cost optimization is medium priority, standard weight

operational_excellence.description: Operational excellence is medium priority, standard weight

sustainability.description: Sustainability is lower priority, weighted 10% lower

critical_gap.description: Missing capabilities (0% coverage) are critical gaps

major_gap.description: Low coverage (<30%) capabilities are major gaps

minor_gap.description: Moderate coverage (30-60%) capabilities are minor gaps

optimization.description: High coverage (60-80%) capabilities can be optimized

high.description: Architecture changes or new patterns, ~1 month

tuning_parameters.description: Parameters that can be tuned in production via hot-reload

notes: Baseline scores set conservatively to ensure meaningful variance
Complexity adjustments prevent over-penalizing large architectures
Score cap at 95% acknowledges that perfection is unrealistic
Pillar priorities reflect typical enterprise concerns
All parameters validated against reference architectures
